mechanism_narrative: A sustained near-zero federal funds rate compresses risk-free discount rates and equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative on average during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8 percent signals excess labor slack and weak household income growth that depresses corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in retail sales and housing starts captures broad-based consumer and investment demand that single-indicator models omit, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is non-positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-industrial-shock recoveries, month-over-month stabilization in industrial production signals operating leverage recovery and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of post-shock quarters during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP falling below 13200 billion chained 2017 dollars signals insufficient aggregate demand to support prior earnings run-rates, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than half of the quarters when GDPC1 is below 13200.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll changes coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero nominal rates and a stable price level generates expectations of prolonged negative real rates, accelerating weekly flows into equity risk assets and producing positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Jointly low FEDFUNDS and moderate CPIAUCSL fail to produce positive average 5-day returns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls below 130000 thousand indicate stalled labor market healing that undermines consumption growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when PAYEMS is below 130000.
